fallback_behavior.default_mode: Read repository-local Markdown entrypoints first and follow their ordered routing into authored content.
when_optional_helpers_are_unavailable: Do not assume Python, package managers, network access, or a service process.
If a local case path is available, collect the minimum manual case snapshot described in CASE_AUTO_INTAKE_SPEC.md.
If required runtime entrypoints are missing, stop and report the repository as incomplete for portable runtime use.

compatibility_policy.contract_intent: This contract defines portable file-based repository consumption for external coding agents.
guarantees: Human-authored Markdown remains the primary runtime knowledge surface.
The repository stays usable even when optional helpers cannot run.
Future generated runtime views may be added, but they must preserve or clearly bridge the current entry behavior before older entrypaths are retired.
change_rules: Avoid breaking existing runtime entrypoint paths without compatibility coverage.
Keep helper execution optional and additive.
Do not redefine the contract as a network API, package-managed dependency, or long-running service.

runtime_surface_policy.default_usage_rule: Start from the listed entrypoints, then follow repository reading order into authored Markdown and routing artifacts.
runtime_surface_policy.exclusion_rule: Project-state, planning, progress, and validation-status documents are outside the default runtime dependency surface.
